title: Integrated data | Stats NZ
content:
## About Integrated Data
Stats NZ collects data from different sources and links it together to create integrated data.
Linking data is a way for us to get maximum benefit for New Zealand from the data we manage. Researchers can apply to use integrated data for cross-sector research projects that are in the public interest.
The data comes from the surveys we run, other government agencies, and non-government organisations (NGOs). It is microdata, which means it's about units of a population, such as individuals, households, or businesses.
We link data records by using the variables they have in common. Then we remove personal information so individuals can't be identified.
Data is protected by laws and other measures that guard your privacy.
Research projects that use integrated data must meet the conditions we've set to keep data safe. We grant access to data only when projects meet all these conditions. Research must be in the public interest and we require projects to publish their findings.
Go to How we keep integrated data safe to learn more.
Go to Research using Stats NZ microdata to browse research projects and publications.

## How our integrated databases work
- We collect data from government agencies, Stats NZ surveys, and NGOs.
- We link records that belong to the same person or business using the variables they have in common.
- We de-identify the data and make it available to researchers for approved research projects.

## Integrated data for producing official statistics
Stats NZ is increasingly using integrated data to produce official statistics.
We have been integrating data with tax-sourced admin data to produce economic statistics for many years.
More recently we have used integrated data to produce social statistics on disability, te ao Māori, and child poverty. We have also used integrated data to produce the 2018 Census dataset.

## Advisory groups and initiatives
Several key groups provide advice and undertake initiatives related to the integrated data service at Stats NZ.

### Integrated Data Services Working Group
The Integrated Data Services Working Group focuses on the future of the integrated data service, providing support and advice on strategic and tactical opportunities to improve it. The group has representation from across the data system.

### Data Lab Action Group
The Data Lab Action Group is a representative body of Data Lab researchers from public and private agencies. As representatives of the technical integrated data research community, they collect and prioritise community concerns and suggestions, provide feedback and advice on integrated data issues, and work on projects that support the work of Data Lab researchers in collaboration with Stats NZ.
Data Lab Action Group – terms of reference

### Code modules initiative
The code modules initiative is a cross-agency effort to make IDI and LBD data easier to use, and more understandable for everyone.
Code modules initiative
Integrated Data Infrastructure
Longitudinal Business Database
